result.lens: ROADMAP Phase-2 reconciliation: which unchecked items are unstarted vs partial vs secretly-done, and which are v1-worthy vs v2-defer (pillars 1+2+3 = v1 core; marketplace deferred-not-deleted; wiki/pillar-5 fenced experimental per assessment)
result.summary: I assessed all eight unchecked ROADMAP Phase-2 items against the code. Three categories emerged. (1) DOC DRIFT — two items are effectively already done and should be checked off, not built: "Adapter-specific instruction scope translation tests" (export-side covered for Cursor/Windsurf/Copilot/Kiro across all 4 scopes in test/core/instructions.test.ts, import-side scope inference covered in cursor/kiro/windsurf import.test.ts), and the related already-[x] "context injection at apply" is genuinely wired (src/core/instructions.ts:164). (2) GENUINELY UNSTARTED but correctly v2-defer for a credible pillar-1+2 v1: LLM-powered extraction (harvester is 100% regex, no model calls anywhere — and an LLM dependency conflicts with the local-first/zero-runtime-dep thesis), embedding semantic search (no cosine/vector code; the embeddings?: number[] field at src/wiki/types.ts:80 is dead and never written/read — a vestigial-field doc-drift smell), Obsidian-style graph-viz HTML export (graph.ts:163 emits only JSON nodes/edges; ROADMAP implies near-done), cross-project knowledge linking (global store + am wiki sync exist but no meta-index), A2A multi-agent orchestration (flows.ts supports only local acp/action/compute/checkpoint nodes — there is NO a2a/remote node type, so remote agent chaining is impossible; but flows are binary-hostile per P2-G so this is a v2 concern), and mDNS/DNS-SD discovery (absent, niche). (3) The ONE genuinely-actionable NEW gap worth surfacing for v1 credibility: the DiffChange type (src/adapters/types.ts:97) advertises "skill" and "agent" drift entities that ZERO adapters ever emit — Claude Code claims skills+agents capabilities yet diff.ts only compares servers+instructions. This is the ROADMAP "Full skill/agent drift detection" item and is distinct from backlog P2-D (which is scoped to instruction-drift coverage). I also found a NEW bug not in the P0/P1/P2 backlog: am wiki graph and am wiki lint declare a --global flag but ignore it (loadGraph()/listPages() called with no wikiDir at wiki.ts:782 and :888, unlike every sibling subcommand). Net: most Phase-2 items are correctly v2-defer; surface only the skill/agent-drift type-vs-reality gap and the wiki --global bug as actionable, and fix two ROADMAP checkboxes that are secretly done.

findings.title: Emit or remove the unused skill/agent DiffChange entity kinds — drift detection silently never fires for skills or agents
findings.evidence: src/adapters/types.ts:97 declares `entity: "server" | "instruction" | "skill" | "agent" | "setting"`, but a repo-wide grep finds ZERO adapters emitting a skill or agent DiffChange. src/adapters/claude-code/diff.ts (which claims skills+agents capabilities per ROADMAP:49) only compares servers (lines 57-91) and instructions (lines 94-106). ROADMAP:227 lists this unchecked as 'Full skill/agent drift detection across all 13 adapters'.
findings.recommendation: This is the ROADMAP skill/agent-drift item and is NOT covered by backlog P2-D (which is explicitly scoped to instruction-drift coverage across 3-of-13 adapters). Pick one: (a) implement skill/agent drift in the handful of adapters that actually export skills/agents (Claude Code, ForgeCode, Kilo Code, Kiro, Codex), reusing the compareInstructions pattern; or (b) if v1 won't ship it, narrow the DiffChange union to the entities actually emitted and downgrade the 'drift detection over overwrite' promise in docs so `am status`/`am apply --diff` does not falsely report skill/agent edits as in-sync. Option (b) is the cheaper honesty fix for v1; (a) is the real feature for v1.x.

findings.title: Fix `am wiki graph --global` and `am wiki lint --global` — flag declared but ignored
findings.evidence: src/commands/wiki.ts:781-782 (lint) calls `listPages()` and `loadGraph()` with no argument, and :888 (graph) calls `loadGraph()` with no argument, despite both declaring `global: {type: "boolean", ...}` at :776 and :879. Every sibling subcommand correctly threads it, e.g. :101 `const wikiDir = args.global ? resolveWikiDir({ global: true }) : undefined;` and :955. Result: `am wiki graph --global` and `am wiki lint --global` silently operate on the project graph/pages, never the global wiki.
findings.recommendation: Thread `args.global` through: compute `const wikiDir = args.global ? resolveWikiDir({ global: true }) : undefined;` and pass it to `loadGraph(wikiDir)` and `listPages(...)` (both already accept an optional wikiDir/baseDir per graph.ts:25 and storage.ts). Add a regression test asserting `--global` reads the global graph. This is a NEW bug not in the P0/P1/P2 backlog.

findings.title: Remove the dead `embeddings?: number[]` wiki field or wire it — vestigial of unbuilt semantic search
findings.evidence: src/wiki/types.ts:80 declares `embeddings?: number[];` on the wiki entry type, but a repo-wide grep (`grep -rn embeddings src/ test/`) finds it is NEVER written or read anywhere. Wiki search is pure MiniSearch/BM25 (src/wiki/storage.ts:703-769). ROADMAP:188 lists 'Embedding-based semantic search (cosine similarity on wiki entries)' as unchecked.
findings.recommendation: Embedding semantic search is correctly v2-defer (it would add a model/embedding runtime dependency that conflicts with the local-first, zero-runtime-dep thesis). But leaving a populated-by-nothing field invites a reader to assume it works. Delete the field (or comment it `// reserved for v2 semantic search, not yet populated`). Pairs with the assessment's doc-honesty theme (P0-4).

findings.title: Check off two ROADMAP Phase-2 items that are already done (instruction-scope tests; mark scope-translation complete)
findings.evidence: ROADMAP:229 'Adapter-specific instruction scope translation tests' is unchecked, but export-side scope translation is fully tested in test/core/instructions.test.ts (Cursor alwaysApply all 4 scopes lines 211-244; Windsurf trigger all 4 scopes 258-284; Copilot 296+; Kiro inclusion all 4 scopes 333-358), and import-side scope inference is tested in test/adapters/cursor/import.test.ts:173,206, kiro/import.test.ts:179, windsurf/import.test.ts:122. The source translators exist at src/core/instructions.ts:225 (Cursor), :241 (scopeToWindsurfTrigger), :277 (Copilot applyTo), :289 (scopeToKiroInclusion).
findings.recommendation: Mark ROADMAP:229 as done (or scope it down to 'add a per-adapter roundtrip scope-preservation test' if you want belt-and-suspenders coverage, which would be the only remaining gap — export and import are each tested but not asserted as a single roundtrip). This is doc drift, not missing work; reconciling it tightens the assessment's stat-honesty pass without writing features.

findings.title: Defer A2A multi-agent orchestration and mDNS to v2 — and add an `a2a`/remote node type to flows only when flows leave experimental
findings.evidence: src/protocols/acp/flows.ts:48 defines `FlowNode = AcpNode | ActionNode | ComputeNode | CheckpointNode` — there is NO a2a/remote/delegate node type, so a flow cannot chain a remote A2A agent (grep for a2a/delegate/remote in flows.ts returns nothing). A2A delegation works one-shot via `am agents delegate` (discovery.ts) but is not composable. mDNS/DNS-SD is entirely absent (no bonjour/multicast/dns-sd references anywhere). ROADMAP:197-198.
findings.recommendation: Keep both deferred for a pillar-1+2 v1. The orchestrator notes pillar 3 (ACP/A2A) is v1 core, but the assessment fences it experimental and flows are binary-hostile (backlog P2-G: flows.ts uses runtime function refs in ComputeNode.fn at :37-38, hostile to `bun build --compile`). Sequencing: resolve P2-G (declarative flows) FIRST, then the A2A node type becomes a small, coherent v1.x add. mDNS is niche LAN-discovery — leave for v2. No new backlog item needed beyond noting the dependency on P2-G.

findings.title: Defer LLM-powered wiki extraction, graph-viz HTML, and cross-project meta-index to v2
findings.evidence: Harvester is 100% regex/heuristic with zero model calls (src/wiki/harvester.ts:131-306 are all regex extractors; grep for openai/anthropic/llm/completion in src/wiki finds only a secret-detection regex). Graph viz: src/wiki/graph.ts:163 `exportGraphForViz` returns only JSON `{nodes, edges}`; no HTML/SVG/d3/mermaid output exists (ROADMAP:189 implies HTML export). Cross-project: global store + `am wiki sync` (wiki.ts:1044) exist but there is no meta-index; ROADMAP:191.
findings.recommendation: All three are correctly unchecked and correctly v2-defer for a pillar-1+2 v1. LLM extraction specifically conflicts with the local-first/zero-runtime-dependency design principle (ROADMAP:22) and the single-binary tenet — it would need an API key and network at harvest time. Graph-viz HTML is low-value polish. Cross-project meta-index depends on the wiki being non-empty (harvest-dependent, per AGENTS.md:43). No actionable v1 item; flagged only to confirm they are NOT secretly done and should remain unchecked rather than aspirationally marked.

result.lens: Committed-docs factual drift sweep (ROADMAP, ADR index, design specs, schema.ts references) vs code at HEAD, post-wave-0, de-duplicated against PRODUCTION-READINESS-ASSESSMENT §5 P0/P1/P2 and BACKLOG-STATE scope overrides
result.summary: ROADMAP.md is the single largest unaddressed drift surface: its "Stats" table and "Implementation Status" sections are a generation behind the code (tests 1,916 vs 3,064; source files 176 vs 215; ADRs 47 vs 54; commands 31 vs 36; MCP tools 33 vs the documented-elsewhere 38) and it presents stale per-feature tables as current "Done" status (ACP built-in "16" pre-ADR-0033-split, "6-file pattern" adapters that no longer have schema.ts, a "Git-Based Marketplace — Complete" section that ignores the v2-DEFERRED decision). None of this is enumerated in the assessment, whose P0-4 only names the README badge/transcript and CLAUDE.md pillar 4. Separately, ADR-0053 (am setup) exists on disk but is missing from BOTH the ADRs/README.md index and AGENTS.md, and AGENTS.md's own count line says "53 records (0001-0052)" when there are 54 numbered ADRs through 0053 — wave 0 fixed the test count in AGENTS.md but not the ADR count it sits next to. Finally, the schema.ts-deletion drift (ADR-0041) lives in two NEW doc surfaces the assessment's P2-E does not cite: the contributor-facing system-architecture.md (lists adapter schema.ts as a real file AND describes a live "Phase 2 — Adapter validation" pass that no longer exists) and the design spec. The dated v0.3.0 design spec is lower-priority (it self-labels as a historical snapshot), but system-architecture.md and ROADMAP.md present themselves as current references. Scope overrides honored: marketplace findings are framed as "reflect DEFERRED, don't delete," and ACP/A2A are treated as in-core.

findings.title: Regenerate ROADMAP.md Stats table — every metric is a generation stale
findings.evidence: ROADMAP.md:313-326 — Source files 176 (actual 215), Test files 152 (actual 231), Tests 1,916 (actual 3,064), Assertions 5,655 (actual 9,672), CLI commands 31 (actual 36 lazy subcommands in src/cli.ts), MCP tools 33 (AGENTS.md says 38 = 33 active + 5 deprecated aliases), ADRs 47 (actual 54 numbered: 0001-0053 + 0031a). Wave 0 fixed README/AGENTS badges but left ROADMAP untouched.
findings.recommendation: Replace the hardcoded Stats table with CI-generated values (the same stat script P0-4 proposes) OR delete the table and link to AGENTS.md as the single source of stats. At minimum update all 7 wrong numbers. This is distinct from P0-4, which only names the README badge and transcript.

findings.title: ROADMAP.md ADR Index claims 47 ADRs and is missing entries 0031-0053 — self-admits being stale
findings.evidence: ROADMAP.md:270-310 — header literally says "47 architectural decisions ... ADRs 0031-0035, 0037-0043, 0045-0046 are present in the repo and not yet mirrored here"; the table stops mirroring at 0036/0044 and omits 0037-0043, 0045-0053. True count is 54 numbered ADRs.
findings.recommendation: Either delete ROADMAP's partial ADR index entirely and point to ADRs/README.md (the canonical index), or regenerate it in full. A self-admittedly-incomplete duplicate index is worse than no index.

findings.title: Add ADR-0053 to the canonical ADR index (ADRs/README.md) — currently stops at 0052
findings.evidence: ADRs/README.md index table last row is 0052 (Marketplace v1 Code Removal Target); ADRs/0053-am-setup-first-run-wizard.md (status: proposed) exists on disk but has no index row. Confirmed: ls ADRs/*.md shows 0053, grep of README.md index shows max entry 0052.
findings.recommendation: Add a row for ADR-0053 (am-setup-first-run-wizard, Proposed, 2026-05-31) to ADRs/README.md. This is the index that P2-F touches for re-statusing but P2-F does not mention the missing 0053 row.

findings.title: Fix AGENTS.md ADR count — says '53 records (0001-0052)' but 54 numbered ADRs exist through 0053
findings.evidence: AGENTS.md:174 — "ADRs/ # 53 architectural decision records (0001-0052, incl. 0031a)". Actual: 54 numbered files (0001-0053 + 0031a; 0053 added 2026-05-31). Wave 0 corrected the adjacent test count (line 173: 231 files, 3064 tests, 9672 assertions — correct) but not the ADR count one line below it.
findings.recommendation: Update to '54 architectural decision records (0001-0053, incl. 0031a)'. AGENTS.md is the canonical doc per the project's own thesis, so its count line must be exact.

findings.title: ROADMAP.md presents 'Git-Based Marketplace — Complete' as current status, contradicting the v2-DEFERRED decision
findings.evidence: ROADMAP.md:216-223 — section header 'Git-Based Marketplace — Complete' with all rows 'Done' and 'CLI (7 subcommands) | Done'; ROADMAP.md:84 lists 'Marketplace: marketplace (7 subcommands)' as a shipped command group. AGENTS.md §pillar-4 and BACKLOG-STATE.md mark marketplace as DEFERRED to v2 (kept, not advertised in v1 CLI surface).
findings.recommendation: Re-frame the ROADMAP marketplace section as 'Deferred to v2' (mirror AGENTS.md pillar-4 language: code retained, paused until hosted web platform). Do NOT delete the section or the code (BACKLOG-STATE supersedes ADR-0039/0052 deletion). Move it out of the 'Complete' shipped-feature framing.

findings.title: ROADMAP.md claims 'ACP built-in (16)' — stale pre-ADR-0033 count; the flat list was split into 4+3+7=14 tiered entries
findings.evidence: ROADMAP.md:182 — 'Unified Agent Registry (ADR-0030) | Done | config > ACP built-in (16) > A2A roster'. ADR-0033 split the flat 16-entry list into tier-1-native (claude/codex/gemini/kiro = 4), tier-2-shim (aider/amazon-q/cody = 3), tier-3-catalog-only (cline/continue/copilot/cursor/kilo-code/roo-code/windsurf = 7) = 14 built-ins. Tier markers in src/core/agent-registry.ts confirm the tiered model.
findings.recommendation: Update the note to reflect the ADR-0033 tiered roster (14 built-ins across 3 tiers, only 4 tier-1 spawnable directly). ACP is in-core per BACKLOG-STATE, so this is an accuracy fix not a fence.

findings.title: ROADMAP.md adapter section says '6-file pattern' but adapters have 5 files (schema.ts deleted per ADR-0041)
findings.evidence: ROADMAP.md:63 — 'All adapters: detect, import, export, drift detection. 6-file pattern.' Zero adapters contain schema.ts (find src/adapters -name schema.ts = 0). ADR-0041 deleted the Adapter.schema field; AGENTS.md:373 already documents this ('the old schema.ts / schema field was deleted per ADR-0041, so do NOT create...').
findings.recommendation: Change to '5-file pattern (index/detect/import/export/diff)'. Related to P2-E but P2-E only cites CLAUDE.md:288 and adapter-development-guide — ROADMAP.md is not in P2-E's evidence.

findings.title: system-architecture.md describes a live 'Phase 2 — Adapter validation' pass and lists adapter schema.ts as a real file — both removed by ADR-0041
findings.evidence: docs/system-architecture.md:330-333 describes 'Phase 2 -- Adapter validation: Each adapter\'s schema property contains Zod schemas... validates its portion'; line 349 lists 'schema.ts -- Zod schemas for adapter-specific TOML fields' in the per-adapter file structure; line 34 (mermaid) shows a Schema/schema.ts node. ADR-0041 deleted the Adapter.schema field (src/adapters/types.ts:168-169 explicitly documents the deletion); no adapter has schema.ts. This is a CURRENT contributor-facing reference, not a dated snapshot.
findings.recommendation: Rewrite the Two-Phase Validation section to a single-phase reality (core validates strictly, adapter sections are opaque passthrough preserved verbatim per ADR-0007/0041) and drop schema.ts from the adapter file-structure listing and mermaid diagram. P2-E does NOT cite system-architecture.md, so this is a new surface.

findings.title: Mark the v0.3.0 design spec as historical and strip its stale schema.ts / '26 tools, 4 groups' references from being mistaken as current
findings.evidence: docs/2026-04-07-agent-manager-design-spec.md:2-4 self-labels Version 0.3.0 / Status 'Updated with ADRs 0018-0024'; line 632 '26 tools across 4 groups', line 903 'JSON-RPC 2.0, 26 tools, 4 groups' (now 38 tools / 6 groups), line 897 adapter 'schema.ts' (deleted ADR-0041), line 7 carries the competing 'chezmoi for AI agent configs' tagline (P0-4 territory).
findings.recommendation: Lowest priority because the doc is explicitly dated. Add a banner ('Historical snapshot at v0.3.0; see AGENTS.md + system-architecture.md for current state') rather than editing the body, so it is not read as a current spec. Do not spend wave-0/1 effort here.

result.lens: CLI UX consistency (--json/--quiet/--verbose, error actionability, exit codes, TTY-safety, help/completion coverage) across the 36 `am` commands
result.summary: I audited all 36 registered subcommands plus their helpers. Most commands correctly re-declare `--json/--quiet/--verbose` and route output through `src/lib/output.ts`, and every command that errors sets an exit code on at least one path. However I found six concrete, verified UX inconsistencies that hurt scripting agents and first users, none of which overlap the known P0/P1/P2 backlog (§5 of PRODUCTION-READINESS-ASSESSMENT.md). The two most damaging are (1) the documented "global" flags `--json/--verbose/--quiet/--profile` on the root command are non-functional — citty does not thread parent args to subcommands, so `am --json version` prints plain text and `am --profile X list` exits 1 with a usage dump; and (2) `am profile delete` and `am wiki resolve` render interactive `@clack/prompts` even under `--json`/closed-stdin, hanging or corrupting output for non-interactive callers. I verified each finding by running the CLI against temp configs (`AM_CONFIG_DIR`) and citing source lines. The known backlog's P1-A (ZodError dump), P1-B (apply mutates without confirm), and P1-G (help omits pair/secrets/mcp-superset) are real but I did NOT re-file them; the completion-script drift below is a distinct surface from P1-G's help gap.

findings.title: Thread root-level global flags (--json/--verbose/--quiet/--profile) into subcommands or document them as positional-after-subcommand only
findings.evidence: src/cli.ts:13-22 declares json/verbose/quiet/profile on `main`, but citty 0.1.6 does not pass parent args to subcommands. Verified: `am --json version` prints `0.0.0-dev` (plain) not JSON (exit 0); `am --profile foo list servers` exits 1 dumping grouped usage. CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md tell users/agents these are global flags.
findings.recommendation: Either (a) add a runMain/argv pre-parse shim that hoists these four flags into each subcommand before citty routes, or (b) delete them from `main.args` and update CLAUDE.md/docs to state flags must follow the subcommand (`am version --json`). Option (a) preserves the documented contract; option (b) at least stops `am --json <cmd>` from silently returning human text with exit 0, which is the worst failure mode for a scripting agent.

findings.title: Skip interactive confirm in `am profile delete` when --json or stdin is not a TTY
findings.evidence: src/commands/profile.ts:228-236 — the only guard is `if (!args.yes)`; the comment claims "skip if --yes flag or non-interactive" but there is no isTTY/args.json check. Verified: `am profile delete foo --json </dev/null` renders a clack prompt emitting `[?25l` escape codes to the terminal instead of JSON, and blocks. Contrast `src/commands/install.ts:91` which correctly guards with `!args.json && process.stdin.isTTY`.
findings.recommendation: Gate the confirm with `if (!args.yes && !args.json && process.stdin.isTTY)`; in non-interactive/JSON mode without --yes, refuse with an actionable error (`error("Refusing to delete profile non-interactively; pass --yes", opts); process.exitCode = 1`) rather than prompting. Match the install.ts pattern.

findings.title: Make `am wiki resolve` non-interactive-safe (auto-skip or require --strategy under --json/no-TTY)
findings.evidence: src/commands/wiki.ts:1216-1227 — `clack.select(...)` is called whenever `--strategy` is absent, with no isTTY/args.json guard. A scripting agent running `am wiki resolve --json` (after a sync conflict) hangs on an interactive picker.
findings.recommendation: When `!process.stdin.isTTY || args.json` and no `--strategy` is supplied, either default each file to `skip` and report the unresolved list in the JSON envelope, or emit an actionable error instructing the caller to pass `--strategy keep-local|take-remote|skip`. Do not enter the clack picker in non-interactive mode.

findings.title: Set non-zero exit code when `am install` fails to fetch or find a package
findings.evidence: src/commands/install.ts:61-70 push `action:"failed"` and `continue` without ever setting `process.exitCode`; the only exit-1 is the outer catch at line 223. Verified: `am install __nonexistent__ --json` prints `{"action":"failed","reason":"not found"}` but exits 0. Sibling `src/commands/uninstall.ts:35` correctly sets `process.exitCode = 1` on not-found — so install is inconsistent with uninstall.
findings.recommendation: After the per-package loop, `if (results.some(r => r.action === "failed")) process.exitCode = 1;`. A scripting agent currently treats a typo'd or unavailable package as a successful install.

findings.title: Generate shell completions from the live command tree instead of hardcoded duplicate lists
findings.evidence: src/commands/completion.ts:16-71 hardcodes TOP_LEVEL_COMMANDS and SUBCOMMANDS that have drifted from src/cli.ts. Missing real commands: `pair`, `secrets`, `mcp-superset` (and aliases `acp`,`agents`). Lists `init-project` which is NOT a registered subcommand (grep of cli.ts confirms it is internal-only). `wiki` subcommand list omits real subcommands `sync`,`resolve`,`path`,`migrate`,`publish`,`pull` (verified against wiki.ts defineCommand keys) and lists nonexistent ones. This is a SEPARATE surface from backlog P1-G (which covers `am --help` only).
findings.recommendation: Derive completion candidates by reflecting over `main.subCommands` keys and each subcommand's `subCommands` at generate time, or add a coverage test asserting completion lists === cli.ts/subcommand keys (mirroring the ADR-0029 help coverage test). Hardcoded mirror lists will keep drifting on every new command.

findings.title: Make parent commands with subcommands exit 0 (not 1) when invoked with no subcommand and standardize exit codes
findings.evidence: Verified `am config`, `am secrets`, `am pair`, `am wiki`, `am session`, `am profile` all print USAGE to stdout but exit 1. `am --help` exits 0; `am status --help` exits 0. Meanwhile `mcp-superset` defines a bespoke 0/1/2/3 exit protocol (src/commands/mcp-superset.ts:15-22) unlike every other command's uniform 0/1. The mixed convention (help-like output but exit 1; one command with a 4-level protocol) makes exit codes unreliable for scripting.
findings.recommendation: Pick one convention: printing usage because a required subcommand is missing is a usage error, so exit 2 (or print to stderr) is defensible — but it should be consistent across all parent commands, and `--help`/`-h` on a parent should exit 0. Document the exit-code contract (0 ok, 1 runtime failure, 2 usage error) in a CONVENTIONS doc and reconcile mcp-superset's richer protocol with it.

result.lens: test-coverage / handler-bypassing tests on shipping CLI commands (pillars 1+2)
result.summary: Systematic audit of every src/commands/*.ts handler vs its test/ counterpart reveals a pervasive, NEW pattern the production-readiness assessment never names: most command tests are handler-BYPASSING. They import core library primitives (loadResolvedConfig, getStatus, revertHead, addRemote, encrypt*) and test those directly, while the actual citty `<cmd>Command.run()` handler — which owns user-facing logic: arg parsing, --json output shape, error messages, process.exitCode branches, drift gates, --force, auto-commit — is never executed. Method: grepped for `<symbol>.run`, `invoke(<symbol>`, `(<symbol> as`, and lazy `await import` patterns across test/, then read the suspect test bodies and handler source to confirm. A working handler-test pattern already exists in the repo (install/uninstall/update/add/version call `Command.run!({args})` correctly), so this is purely missing test authorship, not a harness gap. The assessment's §5/§6 only flags onboarding/`am init`/`am setup` as untested (Wave 3); it never mentions that already-shipping pillar-1/2 commands (apply live path, import brownfield engine, status, use, config, push, pull, undo, list, doctor, secret, profile) have zero handler-level coverage. These are the commands a v1 MUST have tested because they auto-commit to the user's git-backed catalog (ADR-0002), mutate global home-dir IDE configs (apply), encrypt secrets (add/import/secret), and gate destructive overwrites on drift. Corrections to my own first pass: `add` and `version` ARE adequately handler-tested (via lazy `await import` + `.run!`), and `apply` is PARTIALLY tested (dry-run envelope only); I excluded those from the findings except apply's untested live/drift branch.

findings.title: Test am apply's live drift-refusal gate and --force path (handler runs only --dry-run today)
findings.wave: Wave 2 (wizard prerequisite — apply is the wizard's destructive step)
findings.evidence: applyCommand.run is invoked ONLY by test/commands/dry-run-envelope.test.ts:204 with dryRun:true/force:false; the live branches at src/commands/apply.ts:109 (skip drifted adapter), :141-146 (process.exitCode=1 + 'rerun with --force' message), and the --force override are never exercised. test/commands/apply.test.ts re-implements buildResolvedConfig locally (lines 10-36) and only tests loadResolvedConfig/interpolateEnvAsync primitives.
findings.recommendation: Add a handler test that drives applyCommand.run({args:{dryRun:false,diff:true,force:false}}) against a tmpdir with one drifted adapter: assert it skips the drifted target, sets process.exitCode=1, and emits the 'X skipped (drift detected; rerun with --force)' message; then re-run with force:true and assert overwrite. Reuse the invoke() helper already in dry-run-envelope.test.ts.

findings.title: Test am import brownfield-merge handler end-to-end (auto-encrypt + auto-commit never run)
findings.wave: Wave 3 (it is wizard step 3 'RUN import auto' — but the engine ships today and is untested now)
findings.evidence: test/commands/import.test.ts contains 11 extractServerIdentity unit tests + one MCP am_import tool test; importCommand.run() is never invoked. The handler's brownfield merge/conflict logic (src/commands/import.ts:104-186), secret auto-scan+encrypt (:319-341), --yes/--report flags (:56-61), and the error path (:527 process.exitCode=1) have zero coverage despite ADR-0028 making this pillar-1's brownfield engine.
findings.recommendation: Add test/commands/import-auto.test.ts driving importCommand.run({args:{source:'auto',yes:true}}) against a tmp HOME with a fixture native config containing a plaintext secret: assert servers merged + deduped, the secret was scanned and substituted with ${VAR}+encrypted into settings.env, the config auto-committed, and --report mode writes nothing. Use AM_CONFIG_DIR isolation per test/helpers/tmp.ts.

findings.title: Test am status drift-aggregation handler and --json contract
findings.evidence: test/commands/status.test.ts tests the getStatus() git primitive only (imports from src/core/git, no command import); statusCommand.run() is never invoked. The per-adapter drift loop with try/catch (src/commands/status.ts:52-64), the 'drift detected (N changes)' formatting (:110-111), the --json output shape, and the error/exitCode=1 path (:116-118) are untested.
findings.recommendation: Add a handler test invoking statusCommand.run({args:{json:true}}) against a tmpdir with one in-sync and one hand-edited (drifted) adapter; assert the JSON reports per-adapter status:'drifted' with change counts, and that an uninitialized dir yields the AmError + exitCode=1.

findings.title: Test am use profile-switch handler (state.toml write + unknown-profile error)
findings.evidence: test/commands/use.test.ts tests readActiveProfile/writeActiveProfile helpers from src/commands/use directly; useCommand.run() is never invoked. The handler's profile-existence validation that throws AmError on an unknown profile (src/commands/use.ts:38) and the exitCode=1 error path (:53-55) are untested — a user typo silently relies on an unrun branch.
findings.recommendation: Add a handler test: useCommand.run({args:{profile:'work'}}) flips state.toml; useCommand.run({args:{profile:'does-not-exist'}}) sets exitCode=1 and emits the AmError with the list of valid profiles.

findings.title: Test am config validate/show/edit handlers (ZodError UX path that P1-A will fix is invoked here)
findings.wave: Wave 2 (P1-A ZodError branch lands here — needs a handler test to lock it in)
findings.evidence: test/commands/config.test.ts tests ConfigSchema.safeParse + writeConfig/loadResolvedConfig directly (no command import); validateCommand.run()/showCommand.run()/configCommand.run() are never invoked. The handler error branches at src/commands/config.ts:69, :106, :115 (exitCode=1) and :168-176 (edit validation) are untested — this is the exact surface P1-A wants to improve (raw ZodError → human message), and there is no handler test to assert the improved output.
findings.recommendation: Add handler tests for validateCommand.run() on a malformed config (assert the formatted, non-raw-ZodError message + exitCode=1) and showCommand.run({args:{json:true}}); this also gives P1-A a regression anchor.

findings.title: Test am push/pull handlers' no-remote and error branches
findings.evidence: test/commands/push.test.ts and pull.test.ts test git primitives (getStatus/addRemote/commitAll from src/core/git) only; pushCommand.run()/pullCommand.run() are never invoked. The handler guards 'not a git repo' (push.ts:25, pull.ts:23) and 'no remote configured' (push.ts:33, pull.ts:31) plus the catch→exitCode=1 paths (push.ts:63-65, pull.ts:44-46) are untested — these are the New-Machine/sync value-prop commands.
findings.recommendation: Add handler tests invoking pushCommand.run()/pullCommand.run() against (a) an uninitialized dir and (b) an initialized dir with no remote; assert the specific AmError messages and exitCode=1 without needing network.

findings.title: Test am undo handler guards and post-undo drift warning
findings.evidence: test/commands/undo.test.ts tests revertHead/log git primitives directly; undoCommand.run() is never invoked. The 'Cannot read git log' guard (src/commands/undo.ts:30-32), the 'only initial commit exists' guard (:37-38), the failure path (:48-50), and the catalog/IDE drift warning emitted after a successful undo (:55-80) are all untested.
findings.recommendation: Add a handler test: undoCommand.run() on a repo with only the init commit sets exitCode=1 with the 'Nothing to undo' message; on a repo with 2+ commits it creates a revert commit and emits the drift warning.

findings.title: Test am doctor handler --json output and exit-code contract (the wizard's 'done' signal)
findings.wave: Wave 3 (doctor is the wizard's green-finish gate)
findings.evidence: test/commands/doctor.test.ts tests getStatus/listAdapters/ConfigSchema directly; doctorCommand.run() is never invoked. The 11-check inventory assembly (src/commands/doctor.ts:63-265), the aggregate status roll-up, --json emission, and the final exitCode=1-on-fail (:400) are untested — yet the assessment §4.2 designates `am doctor --json` + exit codes as the wizard's completion signal.
findings.recommendation: Add a handler test driving doctorCommand.run({args:{json:true}}) against a known-healthy tmp setup (assert all checks ok, exitCode unset) and a broken one (missing config.toml → a fail check + exitCode=1), asserting the JSON shape downstream tooling/wizard will consume.

findings.title: Test am secret handler subcommands (set/get/scan/--fix; key-missing and not-found errors)
findings.wave: Wave 1 (legacy AES is the v1-supported secrets path per P0-3)
findings.evidence: test/commands/secret.test.ts tests encryption primitives (encryptValue/decryptValue/generateKey) directly; none of the 6+ run() handlers in src/commands/secret.ts are invoked. Untested handler branches include 'No encryption key found' (secret.ts:64, :129), 'Server not found' (:96), 'Secret not found' (:146), and the scan/--fix auto-substitute handler (:272-411). Since P0-3 fences age and makes legacy AES the v1 path, these handlers are squarely in-scope and unexercised.
findings.recommendation: Add handler tests for secretCommand subcommands: set→get roundtrip via run(), get with no key (exitCode=1 + message), get unknown name (exitCode=1), and scan --fix on a config with a plaintext token (assert substitution + encryption).

findings.title: Test am list handler (entity-type validation, --active, --json, empty-state)
findings.evidence: test/commands/list.test.ts tests loadResolvedConfig directly; listCommand.run() is never invoked. The invalid-entity-type throw (src/commands/list.ts:29 'Unknown entity type'), the config-read error branch (:67-68), --active/--json formatting, and exitCode=1 (:87-89) are untested.
findings.recommendation: Add handler tests: listCommand.run({args:{type:'bogus'}}) surfaces the 'Valid types:' error; run({args:{type:'servers',json:true}}) emits the expected array; empty config yields the empty-state message.

findings.title: Test am profile subcommand handlers (list/show/create/delete) — zero invocation
findings.evidence: No test file imports src/commands/profile at all (NO-IMPORTER); profileCommand/profileListCommand/profileShowCommand/profileCreateCommand/profileDeleteCommand are never invoked anywhere in test/. Profile creation is wizard step 5 (assessment §4.3) and defuses the default-passthrough P1-H footgun, yet its create/delete handlers (auto-commit, inheritance validation) have no coverage.
findings.recommendation: Add test/commands/profile.test.ts driving profileCreateCommand.run() (creates a profile + auto-commits), profileShowCommand.run({json:true}), and profileDeleteCommand.run() (including delete-of-default and delete-of-missing error branches).

findings.title: Test am mcp-superset handler exit codes (1/2/3) and --strict
findings.wave: v2-defer (fenced surface, but exit-code contract is consumed by tooling)
findings.evidence: test/commands/mcp-superset.test.ts and mcp-superset-apply-io.test.ts test classifyServer/buildSupersetReport/writeProjectWithSuperset helpers only; mcpSupersetCommand.run() is never invoked. The handler's distinct exit codes — 3 on config-read failure (src/commands/mcp-superset.ts:337), 2/refuse on policy violation (:364), and the --strict downgrade of 2→1 (:375) — are untested.
findings.recommendation: If mcp-superset stays in the v1 surface, add a handler test asserting each exit code (3 unreadable config, refuse path, --strict mapping). Otherwise confirm it is fenced per P2-K and defer.

findings.title: Add a CI guard asserting every non-fenced command handler has at least one .run() test
findings.wave: Wave 2 (cheap regression fence; complements P1-G help-coverage test)
findings.evidence: The handler-bypass pattern recurred across 18+ commands undetected because nothing asserts handlers are exercised. The repo already proves the test is mechanizable: install/uninstall/update/add/version/secrets-rotate call Command.run!({args}). Compare to the existing ADR-0029 help-coverage test that P1-G is fixing — same class of meta-test gap.
findings.recommendation: Add a meta-test that enumerates src/commands/*.ts exported *Command symbols and greps test/ for a `<symbol>.run` / `invoke(<symbol>` / aliased-run invocation, failing CI for any non-fenced command with zero handler invocation. Seed its allowlist with the genuinely-fenced commands (marketplace, serve, tui, mcp-serve, session) so the guard stays honest.

findings.title: Test am completion handler output (bash/zsh/fish scripts must be valid)
findings.evidence: test/commands/completion.test.ts imports src/commands/completion but never invokes completionCommand.run(); the per-shell script generation is untested. A malformed completion script silently breaks a user's shell on `source`.
findings.recommendation: Add a handler test invoking completionCommand.run({args:{shell:'bash'}}) (and zsh/fish) asserting the emitted script contains the expected `complete`/`compdef`/`complete -c` directives and exits 0; reject an unknown shell with exitCode=1.

result.lens: Adversarial scan: race conditions, unhandled rejections, resource leaks, input-validation reaching FS/shell, silently-swallowed errors (src/core, src/commands, src/protocols, src/mcp)
result.summary: Fresh adversarial pass beyond the known P0/P1/P2 backlog. The hardened subsystems (A2A *server* with TTL eviction + history caps + SSE idle/heartbeat timers, atomic-write with fsync + tmp cleanup + symlink resolution, ACP subprocess kill-on-init-failure, MCP handler try/catch) hold up under scrutiny — no new bugs there. The new gaps cluster in three areas the first audit missed: (1) WRITE-PATH PATH TRAVERSAL — entity names are validated only as z.string() (no charset), and 7 of 13 adapters (windsurf/copilot/roo-code/cline/amazon-q/continue/kiro) interpolate the raw name straight into a file path on `am apply`, while cursor is the ONLY adapter that sanitizes. The wiki write path (writePage/readPage/deletePage → pagePath = join(dir, `${slug}.md`)) likewise never sanitizes the slug, and `am wiki import <file>` casts untrusted JSON to KnowledgeEntry[] with no Zod validation and uses any supplied id verbatim. (2) A2A CLIENT TRUST BOUNDARY — discoverAgent() casts a remote response straight to AgentCard with no validation and normalizeUrl() does no scheme/host allowlisting (SSRF + unvalidated-card-into-roster), the mirror image of the well-hardened A2A server. (3) FAIL-OPEN / SILENT SWALLOWS — controller.ts drift gate fails OPEN (overwrites a possibly-drifted native config) if adapter.diff() throws, swallowing the error with no warning; `am wiki import` swallows every per-entry error as a bare `skipped++`; the MCP stdio loop has an unbounded line buffer. Lower-severity: an uncleared setTimeout in the ACP client's timeoutPromise keeps the event loop alive up to initTimeout after a successful connect. All findings are NEW (not in PRODUCTION-READINESS-ASSESSMENT §5); each is cited at file:line.

findings.title: Sanitize entity names before using them as file paths in 7 IDE adapter exports (write-path traversal)
findings.evidence: src/adapters/windsurf/export.ts:153 (skillDir = join(..., name)) & :179 (`${name}.md`) reaching mkdirSync+atomicWriteFileSync at :75-76; src/adapters/copilot/export.ts:218; src/adapters/roo-code/export.ts:133; src/adapters/cline/export.ts:100; src/adapters/amazon-q/export.ts:99; src/adapters/continue/export.ts:195; src/adapters/kiro/export.ts:160. Contrast: src/adapters/cursor/export.ts:150 is the ONLY one that sanitizes via name.replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9_-]/g,'-'). Entity keys are unconstrained: src/core/schema.ts uses z.record(z.string(), ...) for servers/instructions/skills/agents with no charset refine.
findings.recommendation: Add a shared safeName()/safeSlug() helper (lift cursor's `replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9_-]/g,'-')`) and route all per-entity filename construction through it in every adapter export; OR add a `.regex(/^[A-Za-z0-9._-]+$/)` refine on entity-map keys in schema.ts so a name like `../../foo` is rejected at validation time. An instruction/skill named `../../.bashrc` currently escapes the target dir on `am apply`.

findings.title: Sanitize wiki slug/id before building page file paths (write/read/delete traversal)
findings.evidence: src/wiki/storage.ts:409-410 pagePath(slug) = join(pageDir(type, baseDir), `${slug}.md`) with no sanitization; consumed by writePage (:597), readPage (:608), deletePage (:623), and the publish path globalPath = join(globalSubdir, `${slug}.md`) at :384. slug originates from entry.id with no charset check (entryToPage :818 sets slug: entry.id; addEntry :886-894 does not sanitize).
findings.recommendation: Validate/sanitize slug to `^[A-Za-z0-9._-]+$` (reject or replace `/`, `..`) inside pagePath() before any join, so all wiki write/read/delete callers are covered at the choke point. MCP am_wiki_add is safe (uses crypto.randomUUID) but the import path and any direct addEntry caller are not.

findings.title: Validate `am wiki import` JSON with Zod and stop swallowing per-entry errors
findings.evidence: src/commands/wiki.ts:729-737 casts untrusted JSON directly to KnowledgeEntry[] (`entries = data as KnowledgeEntry[]`) with no schema validation; :748-750 uses a caller-supplied `entry.id` verbatim (drives slug → file path, see traversal finding); :752-757 `try { await addEntry(entry); imported++ } catch { skipped++ }` swallows EVERY error (traversal write failure, schema mismatch, storage exception) with zero diagnostic.
findings.recommendation: Parse each entry through a KnowledgeEntry Zod schema; reject (don't silently skip) invalid entries with a per-entry reason; never honor an externally-supplied id without sanitization. At minimum, collect and report the swallowed error messages instead of a bare `skipped` count.

findings.title: Drift gate fails OPEN when adapter.diff() throws — silently overwrites possibly-drifted native config
findings.evidence: src/core/controller.ts:274-289 — if adapter.diff() throws, the catch at :285 sets driftSummary=undefined and leaves skipDueToDrift=false, so export() proceeds and overwrites the native file. The error is swallowed with no warning surfaced to the user. This defeats the ADR-0006 drift-protection promise on any runtime diff failure.
findings.recommendation: On diff() throw, fail CLOSED (treat as drifted / skip overwrite unless --force) OR at minimum push a warning into the result so JSON consumers and humans see that drift protection was bypassed. Distinct from P2-D (diff wired in only 3/13 adapters) — this is runtime throw → silent fail-open.

findings.title: A2A client trusts remote Agent Cards without validation and has no URL scheme allowlist (SSRF / unvalidated-into-roster)
findings.evidence: src/protocols/a2a/client.ts:126 `const card = (await resp.json()) as AgentCard` — remote response cast to AgentCard with NO Zod validation, then returned to discovery and persisted to agents.toml (src/protocols/a2a/discovery.ts:81-101). normalizeUrl (client.ts:74-76) only strips trailing slashes — no scheme/host check, so any base (incl. internal hosts) is fetched. Mirror image of the heavily-hardened A2A server (Zod, dedup, TTL).
findings.recommendation: Add an AgentCardSchema (Zod) and parse the fetched card before returning/persisting; enforce an http(s)-only scheme allowlist in normalizeUrl/discoverAgent; consider a response-size cap on resp.json(). Pillar 3 is already fenced-experimental, so a fence note suffices for v1 with the proper fix deferred.

findings.title: Bound the MCP stdio read buffer (unbounded line accumulation)
findings.evidence: src/mcp/server.ts:3188-3192 — `buffer += decoder.decode(chunk, {stream:true})` inside the for-await loop with no max-length guard; a peer that streams bytes without a newline grows `buffer` without bound (OOM). Matters most for the bearer-auth network gateway mode (pillar 2).
findings.recommendation: Enforce a max line/buffer length (e.g. a few MB); on overflow emit a -32700/-32600 error and reset the buffer rather than accumulating indefinitely.

findings.title: ACP client timeoutPromise never clears its setTimeout — keeps event loop alive after successful connect
findings.evidence: src/protocols/acp/client.ts:616-620 timeoutPromise() creates a setTimeout that is never cleared; used in the Promise.race at :218-231. When initialize() wins the race (success case), the timer stays armed for up to initTimeout (10s default). Same uncleared-timer pattern in the bridge (src/protocols/bridge.ts:181-183) and run (src/commands/run.ts:632-635), but those are bounded by client.disconnect() in finally.
findings.recommendation: Return a {promise, cancel} pair (or use AbortSignal.timeout) and clearTimeout in a finally after the race settles, so `am run`/connect exits promptly instead of lingering up to initTimeout. Cosmetic for long-lived servers; user-visible as exit latency for one-shot CLI runs.
